invites.note: Hi Simon! Noticed we share an interest in AI and tech innovation in the Nordics. Would love to connect.
invites.operatorNote: Approved test contact per AGENTS.md safety rules

posts.text:
Something I've been thinking about lately: the gap between AI demos and actual daily use.

Every week there's a new tool that promises to revolutionize your workflow. But in my experience helping our team at Signikant adopt AI tools, the ones that stick aren't the flashiest — they're the ones that solve a real, specific annoyance.

Our best AI win so far? Automated meeting briefings. Not glamorous. Not going viral on Twitter. But it saves our Director 10+ minutes per meeting, every single day.

The lesson: start with the pain point, not the technology.

What's the most surprisingly mundane AI use case that's actually transformed your work? Would love to hear examples.

#AIinBusiness #ProductivityTools #FutureOfWork
posts.operatorNote: First post — AI tools in practice, engagement-driving question

posts.text:
Copenhagen's tech scene doesn't get enough credit.

We don't have the loudest voices or the biggest launches. But what we do have is a culture of building things that actually work. Practical, privacy-conscious, human-centered.

In the past month alone, I've met founders working on everything from AI-powered logistics optimization to sustainable supply chain transparency. And they're not just pitching — they're shipping.

Proud to be part of this ecosystem. If you're building something interesting in the Nordics, I'd love to hear about it.

#CopenhagenTech #NordicInnovation #TechCommunity
posts.operatorNote: Second post — Copenhagen tech scene, community building

posts.text:
Unpopular opinion: the most important skill for an executive assistant in 2026 isn't AI fluency — it's knowing when NOT to use AI.

Some things still need a human touch. A carefully worded email to a frustrated client. Reading the room in a tense meeting. Knowing that your boss needs coffee, not another dashboard.

AI handles the repetitive stuff brilliantly. But the job is ultimately about judgment, empathy, and anticipation. No prompt can replace that.

What's one part of your job that you'll never hand off to AI?

#ExecutiveAssistant #FutureOfWork #HumanSkills
posts.operatorNote: Third post — human skills vs AI, thought leadership angle

newThreads.text: Hey Simon! Just wanted to reach out and say hello. I see you're working in AI and innovation in the Nordics too — always great to connect with people in the space. How's the work at POWER International going?
